v2_sources.title: Ramucirumab plus paclitaxel versus placebo plus paclitaxel in patients with previously treated advanced gastric or gastro-oesophageal junction adenocarcinoma (RAINBOW): a double-blind, randomised phase 3 trial
v2_sources.citation: Ramucirumab plus paclitaxel versus placebo plus paclitaxel in patients with previously treated advanced gastric or gastro-oesophageal junction adenocarcinoma (RAINBOW): a double-blind, randomised phase 3 trial. The Lancet Oncology. 2014.

boundary_tags.population_or_model: patients with previously treated advanced gastric or gastro-oesophageal junction adenocarcinoma

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Overall survival was significantly longer in the ramucirumab plus paclitaxel group than in the placebo plus paclitaxel group (median 9.6 months [95% CI 8.5–10.8] vs 7.4 months [95% CI 6.3

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Median progression-free survival was significantly longer with ramucirumab plus paclitaxel than with placebo plus paclitaxel (4.4 months [95% CI 4.2–5.3] vs 2.9 months [2.8–3.0]; HR 0.635
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Median progression-free survival was significantly longer with ramucirumab plus paclitaxel than with placebo plus paclitaxel (4.4 months [95% CI 4.2–5.3] vs 2.9 months [2.8–3.0]; HR 0.635

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Objective response rate was significantly higher in the ramucirumab plus paclitaxel group (92 [28%] of 330 vs 54 [16%] of 335; p=0.0001)', 'page': 6}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Objective response rate was significantly higher in the ramucirumab plus paclitaxel group (92 [28%] of 330 vs 54 [16%] of 335; p=0.0001)', 'page': 6}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Disease control rate was significantly higher in the ramucirumab plus paclitaxel group (264 [80%] vs 213 [64%]; p<0.0001)', 'page': 6}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Disease control rate was significantly higher in the ramucirumab plus paclitaxel group (264 [80%] vs 213 [64%]; p<0.0001)', 'page': 6}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Grade 3 or higher adverse events more common in ramucirumab plus paclitaxel group included neutropenia (41% vs 19%), leucopenia (17% vs 7%), hypertension (14% vs 2%), fatigue (12% vs 5%),
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Grade 3 or higher adverse events more common in ramucirumab plus paclitaxel group included neutropenia (41% vs 19%), leucopenia (17% vs 7%), hypertension (14% vs 2%), fatigue (12% vs 5%),

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'The combination of ramucirumab with paclitaxel significantly increases overall survival compared with placebo plus paclitaxel, and could be regarded as a new standard second-line treatmen
